source_citation: Dayan et al. 2010 Biochim Biophys Acta 1804:1548-1556 (kinetics + MD); mutation orig. Patzoldt et al. 2006 PNAS 103:12329-12334
notes: Accessions used directly in Dayan 2010's own Methods section - primary pair for the validation gate. Alternative account (Hao et al. 2009, J Phys Chem B 113:4865-4875, DOI 10.1021/jp807442n): loss of interchain Gly210-Ser424 H-bond - CONFIRMED directly from Hao 2009's own abstract (not just Dayan's summary of it). Weight Dayan 2010 as primary per DECISION_LOG; Hao 2009 is purely computational, no wet-lab kinetics.

source_citation: Patzoldt et al. 2006 PNAS 103:12329-12334; same mechanism as pair1 per Dayan 2010
notes: Second biological replicate pair from original Patzoldt 2006 deposit; not a duplicate/conflict with pair1. Background diffs vs pair1 WT: S476C (pair2 only) not implicated in resistance.

Resistant allele carries 3 substitutions (S68N, V361A, R480T); paper's site-directed mutagenesis showed V361A alone is causal, R480T alone is proven non-causal, S68N is background variation. Mechanism class not yet resolved from a structural paper - flagged open, do not infer.

source_citation: Rangani et al. 2019 Front Plant Sci 10:568
notes: R1 resistant genotype carries G399A plus background polymorphisms (E38D S68N R377G K432N) relative to S3 susceptible reference - none of the background positions implicated as causal.

source_citation: Rangani et al. 2019 Front Plant Sci 10:568
notes: R2 resistant genotype: clean single-position difference from S3 at position 399 (G->A) - cleanest confirmation of G399A in this dataset.

source_citation: Giacomini et al. 2017 Pest Manag Sci 73:1559-1563
notes: OPEN ITEM, CONFIRMED via full-text read (not just visible-text search): no GenBank accessions appear anywhere in Giacomini et al. 2017, including References/Acknowledgements. Sequencing was cDNA-based on individual plants (populations GPW/LPW/SPW resistant, KPW sensitive; Table 1 gives per-plant genotypes), not deposited full-length sequences. R98L (a different substitution at the homologous site) belongs to Ambrosia artemisiifolia (common ragweed), confirmed verbatim in the paper's own text. Mechanism detail (H-bond disruption with acifluorfen/fomesafen/sulfentrazone) still cited from Hao et al. 2014 JAFC, not yet read directly. Do not resolve accessions by inference; needs supplementary data or author correspondence.
